name: omarchy_dotfiles
content:
Dotfies 
Omarchy is primariy configured through the so-caed dotfies that ive in ~/.config .
Those are considered your fies for your changes. The fies that ive in ~/.local/share/
omarchy beong to Omarchy itsef, and you ideay shoudn't be messing with those. If you
need to change anything in ~/.local/share/omarchy, you shoud be overwriting the vaue
in ~/.config  instead.
Amost everything can be edited through Setup > Configs > [process] through the
Omarchy menu (Super + Alt + Space). When you do it this way, any process that needs
restarting after config edits automaticay wi be after you quit the Neovim editor (:wq ,
remember!.
Here's a ist of the key fies in ~/.config  and what they contro:
Fie Purpose
~/.config/hypr/
hyprland.conf
Contros keybindings, defaut apps, and everything Hyprand.
Learn more about Hyprand configs.
~/.config/hypr/
monitors.conf Contros your monitors, resoution, and position.
~/.config/hypr/
hypridle.conf Contros your ide/seep settings. Shoudn't need touching.
~/.config/hypr/
hyprlock.conf
Contros your ock screen, but this is syminked to your theme
for stying.
~/.config/waybar/
config.jsonc
Contros your top bar that's run with waybar. Learn more
about Waybar configs.
~/.config/waybar/
style.css Contros your top bar design, but it's syminked to your theme.
~/.config/walker/
config.toml Contros your auncher that's run with Waker.
~/.config/ghostty/
config Contros your termina. Learn more about Ghostty configs.
~/.config/uwsm/
default
Contros your defaut $EDITOR. Requires reaunching
Hyprand when changed.
~/.XCompose
Defines your quick-access emoji and name/emai
autocompete. Make sure to run omarchy-restart-xcompose
after making changes.
If you end up making a ot of changes to tweak your own setup, it's a good idea to backup
a these dotfies. Stow is a great way to do that
Dotfiles · The Omarchy Manual · DHH https://learn.omacom.io/2/the-omarchy-manual/65/dotfiles
1 of 2 2/2/26, 1:26 AM

---

a these dotfies. Stow is a great way to do that.
Adding your own she exports, functions, and aiases 
Omarchy ships with a bunch of ergonomic aiases and hepfu functions, but it's very
common to want to add your own. You shoud add both aiases, functions, and exports in
~/.bashrc . This fie wi not be overwritten on updates. If you want to change any of the
Omarchy defauts, you can aso safey add them here.
Changing interna Omarchy fies 
Look, this is your computer. You can do whatever you want with it, but I woud advise
against making changes to the fies in ~/.local/share/omarchy directy. It' make it harder
for you to upgrade in the future. You're better off just overwriting any defaut vaues you
don't ike in the ~/.config��  foder instead.
You can change just about everything that way, ike the defaut keybindings. Just edit
~/.config/hypr/hyprland.conf to, say, repace Obsidian with Jopin (insta with yay -S
joplin-bin):
bind = SUPER SHIFT, O, exec, joplin
If you insist on changing interna Omarchy fies, you' need to commit your changes
before you can use omarchy-update . You can do that with gcam "Look ma! I'm breaking
the rules!" from inside the ~/.local/share/omarchy directory. Ain't nobody here to te
you what to do!
Dotfiles · The Omarchy Manual · DHH https://learn.omacom.io/2/the-omarchy-manual/65/dotfiles
